outcome: Shocked the Muslim world and led Saudi Arabia to dramatically increase religious conservatism and global Islamic funding

title: Soviet Union invades Afghanistan
description: Soviet forces invaded Afghanistan to support the communist government against mujahideen insurgency. The invasion triggered a decade-long war that drew Muslim fighters from across the Islamic world with American and Saudi support.
sources: Barnett Rubin, The Fragmentation of Afghanistan
outcome: The Afghan jihad radicalized Islamic militancy globally and contributed to the Soviet Union's collapse, but devastated Afghanistan for decades.

title: Iraq invades Iran
description: Saddam Hussein's Iraq invades revolutionary Iran, beginning an eight-year war that would claim over a million lives. The conflict pitted Iraqi Ba'athist nationalism against Iranian Islamic revolutionary ideology.
outcome: Devastated both nations economically and militarily while intensifying Sunni-Shia tensions.

title: Deaths in 1981 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 1981 CE: Hamka — scholar (Philosopher of Agam) in Agam; Muhammad Husayn Tabatabaei — philosopher (Allameh Tabataba'i) in Tabriz.
outcome: 2 notable figures died this year.

title: Assassination of Anwar Sadat
description: Egyptian President Anwar Sadat was assassinated by Islamic Jihad members during a military parade in Cairo. The assassins opposed his peace treaty with Israel and secular policies.
sources: David Hirst and Irene Beeson, Sadat
outcome: Sadat's death marked growing Islamist militancy in Egypt and ended the Camp David era.

title: Deaths in 1982 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 1982 CE: Abdulbaki Golpinarli — scholar (Leading Turkish scholar of Sufism and Mevlevi literature) in Istanbul.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: Deaths in 1983 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 1983 CE: Idris I of Libya — ruler (King Idris I — First and Last King of Libya) in Jaghbub / Benghazi, Libya.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: Deaths in 1984 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 1984 CE: Reza Zanjani — scholar (Mystic of Zanjan) in Zanjan.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: Deaths in 1986 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 1986 CE: Ismail al-Faruqi — scholar (Pioneer of Islamization of Knowledge) in Jaffa (Palestine) / Philadelphia; Ayatollah Abdul Rahman Heidari Ilami — scholar (Mystic of Ilam Province) in Ilam Province.
outcome: 2 notable figures died this year.

title: Deaths in 1988 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 1988 CE: Fazlur Rahman Malik — scholar (Greatest Islamic Modernist Thinker of the 20th Century) in Hazara (Pakistan) / Chicago.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: Palestinian Declaration of Independence
description: The Palestinian National Council, meeting in Algiers, declared the establishment of the State of Palestine with Yasser Arafat as president. The declaration invoked UN Resolution 181 and implicitly recognized Israel.
outcome: Over 100 nations recognized the Palestinian state, advancing Palestinian diplomatic standing.

title: Palestinian Declaration of Independence
description: The Palestine National Council meeting in Algiers declares the establishment of the State of Palestine with Jerusalem as its capital, while implicitly recognizing Israel. Yasser Arafat's declaration marked a shift toward diplomatic engagement.
outcome: Established Palestinian statehood claim and opened path to Oslo negotiations.

title: Deaths in 1989 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 1989 CE: Ayatollah Khomeini — ruler (Supreme Leader — Founder of the Islamic Republic of Iran) in Khomein / Najaf / Tehran.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: Soviet withdrawal from Afghanistan — mujahideen victorious
description: The Soviet Union completes its withdrawal from Afghanistan after a decade-long war that killed over a million Afghans. The mujahideen victory is seen across the Muslim world as a triumph of faith over a superpower.
outcome: Emboldened the global jihad movement and produced a generation of fighters who dispersed across the Muslim world

title: Fatwa against Salman Rushdie issued
description: Ayatollah Khomeini issued a fatwa calling for the death of author Salman Rushdie over The Satanic Verses, which many Muslims considered blasphemous. The decree sparked global controversy over free speech and religious sensitivity.
outcome: The controversy highlighted tensions between Islamic values and Western free expression, influencing debates for decades.

title: Deaths in 1990 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 1990 CE: Nasir Subhani — scholar (Islamic Theology Scholar of Lucknow) in Lucknow.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: Deaths in 1992 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 1992 CE: Muhammad Asad — scholar (Islamic Theology Scholar of Lviv) in Lviv.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: Destruction of Babri Masjid
description: Hindu nationalists demolished the 16th-century Babri Mosque in Ayodhya, claiming it stood on the birthplace of Lord Rama. The destruction sparked riots across India killing over 2,000 people, mostly Muslims.
sources: A.G. Noorani, The Babri Masjid Question
outcome: The event marked a turning point in Hindu-Muslim relations and the rise of Hindu nationalism in India.

title: Bosnian Muslims face genocide in Yugoslav wars
description: Following Bosnia's independence declaration, Bosnian Serb forces launched ethnic cleansing campaigns against Bosnian Muslims (Bosniaks). The Srebrenica massacre in 1995 killed over 8,000 Muslim men and boys in Europe's worst atrocity since World War II.
sources: Noel Malcolm, Bosnia: A Short History
outcome: International intervention ended the war in 1995, but the genocide traumatized European Muslims and exposed Western indifference to Muslim suffering.

title: Bosnian War begins with siege of Sarajevo
description: Serbian nationalist forces led by Radovan Karadžić began the siege of Sarajevo and systematic ethnic cleansing of Bosnian Muslims (Bosniaks), including the 1995 Srebrenica massacre of over 8,000 Muslim men and boys. Bosnian President Alija Izetbegović led resistance against Serbian and Croatian partition plans.
sources: Noel Malcolm, Bosnia: A Short History
outcome: The Bosnian genocide marked Europe's worst atrocity since World War II and revealed international failure to protect Muslim populations in the post-Cold War era.

title: Deaths in 1993 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 1993 CE: Hashim Larijani — scholar (Mirza Hashim Amoli) in Pardameh; Mohammed Aziz Lahbabi — philosopher (Islamic Philosophy Philosopher of Fez) in Fez; Zaki Naguib Mahmoud — philosopher (Egyptian logical positivist philosopher) in Cairo.
outcome: 3 notable figures died this year.

title: Deaths in 1995 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 1995 CE: Mehdi Bazargan — scholar (First Prime Minister of Islamic Republic — Islamic Liberal Thinker) in Tehran, Iran.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: Deaths in 1996 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 1996 CE: Abdus Salam — scientist (First Muslim Nobel Laureate in Physics) in Jhang / London / Trieste.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: Deaths in 1997 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 1997 CE: Amin Ahsan Islahi — scholar (Pakistani Quran commentator and student of Hamiduddin Farahi) in Lahore.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: Deaths in 1998 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 1998 CE: Frithjof Schuon — Sufi mystic (Shaykh 'Isa Nur al-Din) in Basel / Lausanne / Indiana; Mohamed Hamouda Bensai — philosopher (Philosopher of Batna) in Batna; Mohammad-Taqi Ja'fari — philosopher (Islamic Philosophy Philosopher of Tabriz) in Tabriz; Nizar Qabbani — poet (Most widely read modern Arab poet and champion of women's liberation) in Damascus.
outcome: 4 notable figures died this year.

title: Deaths in 1999 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 1999 CE: Abd al-Aziz Ibn Baz — scholar (Ibn Baz or Bin Baz) in Riyadh; Ahmad Azari Qomi — scholar (Founder of the conservative Resalat newspaper) in Qom; Karrar Hussain — philosopher (Philosopher of Kota) in Kota; Abul Hasan Ali Hasani Nadwi — scholar (Indian Islamic scholar and author of Madha Khasira al-Alam) in Lucknow.
outcome: 4 notable figures died this year.

title: September 11 attacks and US invasion of Afghanistan
description: Al-Qaeda operatives hijack four commercial airliners and attack the World Trade Center and Pentagon, killing nearly 3,000 people. The United States invades Afghanistan to destroy al-Qaeda and remove the Taliban government.
outcome: Triggered the global 'War on Terror' that reshaped the Muslim world and Muslim life in the West

title: Destruction of the Bamiyan Buddhas by Taliban
description: The Taliban, led by Mullah Omar, destroyed the monumental 6th-century Buddha statues in Bamiyan Valley, Afghanistan, declaring them idolatrous. The act drew international condemnation and highlighted tensions between cultural heritage preservation and extremist interpretations of Islamic law.
outcome: The destruction became a symbol of cultural vandalism and Taliban extremism.

title: Siege of Herat ends with Taliban defeat
description: During the US-led invasion of Afghanistan, Northern Alliance forces led by Ismail Khan recaptured Herat from the Taliban in November 2001. The city, a historic center of Persian-Islamic culture, fell after a brief siege as Taliban forces collapsed across western Afghanistan.
outcome: Ismail Khan became governor of Herat province and began reconstruction of the war-damaged city.

title: Karzai elected interim leader of Afghanistan
description: Hamid Karzai was selected by the Bonn Conference to lead Afghanistan's interim administration following the fall of the Taliban. He became the first democratically elected president of Afghanistan in 2004.
outcome: Karzai's leadership marked Afghanistan's transition to post-Taliban governance under international oversight.

title: Karzai elected interim leader of Afghanistan
description: Hamid Karzai was selected by the Bonn Conference to lead Afghanistan's interim administration following the fall of the Taliban. He became the first democratically elected president in 2004, serving until 2014.
outcome: Karzai's leadership marked Afghanistan's transition toward democratic governance after decades of conflict.

title: Deaths in 2002 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 2002 CE: Muhammad Hamidullah — scholar (Imam of Hyderabad) in Hyderabad / Paris; Jaun Elia — poet (Philosopher of Amroha) in Amroha.
outcome: 2 notable figures died this year.

title: Gujarat riots and anti-Muslim violence
description: Communal violence in Gujarat, India, killed over 1,000 people, mostly Muslims, following the Godhra train burning incident. Chief Minister Narendra Modi faced criticism for alleged inadequate response to protect Muslim communities during the riots.
outcome: The violence deepened Hindu-Muslim tensions in India and had lasting political ramifications.

title: Bali bombings target Western tourists
description: Jemaah Islamiyah operatives including Imam Samudra and Amrozi bin Nurhasyim detonated bombs in Kuta, killing 202 people, mostly Australian tourists. The attack was Indonesia's deadliest terrorist incident and prompted international counterterrorism cooperation in Southeast Asia.
outcome: The bombings led to major counterterrorism reforms in Indonesia and the arrest of Jemaah Islamiyah leadership.

title: US-led invasion of Iraq
description: A US-led coalition invades Iraq and topples Saddam Hussein, triggering sectarian civil war, the destruction of Iraqi civil society and the eventual rise of ISIS.
sources: Patrick Cockburn, The Rise of Islamic State
outcome: Destabilised Iraq and the wider Middle East, fuelling sectarian conflict and extremist movements

title: Shirin Ebadi wins Nobel Peace Prize
description: Iranian lawyer and human rights activist Shirin Ebadi became the first Muslim woman to win the Nobel Peace Prize for her work on democracy, human rights, and children's and women's rights. Her award highlighted tensions between Islamic jurisprudence and international human rights frameworks.
outcome: Ebadi's recognition brought international attention to Iranian civil society and women's rights movements.

title: Death of Ahmad Yasin, Hamas founder
description: Sheikh Ahmad Yasin, founder and spiritual leader of Hamas, was assassinated by Israeli forces in a targeted airstrike in Gaza. His death sparked widespread protests across the Muslim world.
outcome: Yasin's assassination intensified Palestinian resistance movements and Hamas political consolidation.

title: Deaths in 2004 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 2004 CE: Muhammad Alawi al-Maliki — Sufi mystic (Muhammad ibn Alawi al-Maliki) in Mecca.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: Death of Yasser Arafat
description: Palestinian leader Yasser Arafat died in Paris under controversial circumstances after decades leading the Palestinian national movement. His death created a leadership transition to Mahmoud Abbas and marked the end of an era in Palestinian politics.
outcome: Arafat's death reshaped Palestinian leadership and the peace process dynamics.

title: Beslan school siege by Chechen militants
description: Chechen separatists led by Nur-Pashi Kulayev seized School Number One in Beslan, North Ossetia, taking over 1,100 hostages including 777 children. The siege ended with Russian security forces storming the building, resulting in 334 deaths including 186 children.
outcome: The tragedy intensified Russian counterinsurgency operations in the North Caucasus and global debate about Chechen separatism.

title: Madrid train bombings by al-Qaeda-inspired cell
description: Ten coordinated bombings on commuter trains in Madrid killed 193 people and wounded over 2,000. The attacks were carried out by an Islamist militant cell inspired by al-Qaeda, leading to Spain's withdrawal from Iraq.
outcome: The attacks influenced Spanish elections and led to the withdrawal of Spanish troops from Iraq.

title: Death of Ahmad Yasin, Hamas founder
description: Sheikh Ahmad Yasin, the wheelchair-bound founder and spiritual leader of Hamas, was killed by an Israeli helicopter strike in Gaza City. His assassination sparked widespread protests across the Muslim world.
outcome: Yasin's death elevated him to martyrdom status among Palestinians and strengthened Hamas's resolve.

title: Madrid train bombings by al-Qaeda-inspired cell
description: Coordinated bombings on Madrid commuter trains killed 193 people and wounded over 2,000, carried out by an Islamist militant cell. The attacks occurred three days before Spanish elections and led to Spain's withdrawal from Iraq.
outcome: The bombings became Europe's deadliest terrorist attack since Lockerbie and altered Spanish foreign policy.

title: Deaths in 2005 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 2005 CE: Ahmed Deedat — scholar (Islamic Dawah Pioneer — Interfaith Debater) in Surat (India) / Durban, South Africa; Martin Lings — scholar (Abu Bakr Siraj al-Din) in London / Cairo; Mustafa al-Aqqad — scholar (Director of The Message and Omar Mukhtar — Islamic Cinema Pioneer) in Aleppo / Hollywood; Allama Seyyed jalal al-addin Ashtiani — philosopher (Philosopher of Ashtian) in Ashtian; Ghulam Mustafa Khan — philosopher (Philosopher of British Raj) in British Raj.
outcome: 5 notable figures died this year.

title: Amman Message unites Muslim scholars on interfaith respect
description: 200 of the world's leading Muslim scholars from 50 countries issue the Amman Message, formally recognising eight schools of Islamic jurisprudence and declaring takfir (excommunication) against fellow Muslims impermissible.
outcome: Produced the broadest consensus statement by Muslim scholars in modern history, repudiating extremist theology

title: London bombings by British Muslim extremists
description: Four British Muslim suicide bombers attacked London's public transport system, killing 52 people and injuring hundreds. The attacks, led by Mohammad Sidique Khan, shocked Britain and raised questions about homegrown radicalization in Western Muslim communities.
outcome: The attacks intensified debates about integration, surveillance, and counter-radicalization in Europe.

title: King Abdullah of Saudi Arabia initiates reforms
description: Abdullah bin Abdulaziz became King of Saudi Arabia and initiated gradual reforms including municipal elections, educational modernization, and the King Abdullah University of Science and Technology. His reign marked a careful attempt to balance religious conservatism with technological development.
outcome: Abdullah's reforms laid groundwork for later Vision 2030 initiatives while maintaining Wahhabi religious establishment's influence.

title: Mahmoud Abbas elected President of Palestinian Authority
description: Mahmoud Abbas won the Palestinian presidential election following Yasser Arafat's death, becoming leader of the Palestinian Authority. He represented a more moderate stance than his predecessor.
outcome: Abbas's election marked a shift toward renewed peace negotiations with Israel.

title: Mahmoud Abbas elected President of Palestinian Authority
description: Mahmoud Abbas won the Palestinian presidential election following Yasser Arafat's death, gaining 62% of the vote. He pledged to pursue peace negotiations with Israel while maintaining Palestinian national rights.
outcome: Abbas's election provided continuity for Palestinian leadership but faced challenges from Hamas's growing popularity.

title: Deaths in 2006 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 2006 CE: Ahmad Nadeem Qasmi — poet (Philosopher of Angah) in Angah.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: Execution of Saddam Hussein
description: Former Iraqi president Saddam Hussein was executed by hanging after being convicted of crimes against humanity by an Iraqi tribunal. His execution on Eid al-Adha sparked controversy across the Muslim world about timing and sectarian dimensions.
outcome: The execution deepened Iraq's sectarian divisions and Sunni-Shia tensions.

title: Muhammad Yunus wins Nobel Peace Prize
description: Bangladeshi economist Muhammad Yunus and Grameen Bank received the Nobel Peace Prize for pioneering microfinance and microcredit systems. Yunus's work demonstrated Islamic principles of social justice through economic empowerment of the poor.
outcome: Yunus's recognition elevated Islamic economics and social entrepreneurship globally.

title: Al-Askari Mosque bombing triggers Iraqi sectarian war
description: Unknown attackers destroyed the golden dome of the Al-Askari Mosque in Samarra, one of Shia Islam's holiest sites. The bombing triggered massive sectarian violence between Sunni and Shia Iraqis that killed tens of thousands over the following years.
outcome: The bombing marked the beginning of Iraq's civil war phase and deepened the Sunni-Shia divide across the Middle East.

title: Hamas wins Palestinian legislative elections
description: Hamas won a majority in the Palestinian Legislative Council elections, defeating Fatah in a democratic vote. The victory led to international sanctions and eventual Hamas control of Gaza.
outcome: Hamas's electoral victory deepened Palestinian political divisions and led to the Gaza-West Bank split.

title: Hamas wins Palestinian legislative elections
description: Hamas won a majority in Palestinian Legislative Council elections, defeating Fatah and shocking international observers. The victory led to Western sanctions and eventual violent split with Fatah.
outcome: The election deepened Palestinian political division and led to Hamas's control of Gaza from 2007.

title: A Common Word interfaith initiative launched
description: 138 Muslim scholars including Prince Ghazi bin Muhammad of Jordan and Grand Mufti Mustafa Ceric issued 'A Common Word Between Us and You' as an open letter to Christian leaders. The document proposed a theological basis for Muslim-Christian dialogue centered on love of God and neighbor.
sources: Yale Center for Faith and Culture archives
outcome: The initiative sparked ongoing Muslim-Christian theological dialogue and conferences at major universities worldwide.

title: Benazir Bhutto assassinated in Pakistan
description: Former Pakistani Prime Minister Benazir Bhutto was assassinated in Rawalpindi during an election campaign rally. Her death shocked the Muslim world and destabilized Pakistani politics.
outcome: Bhutto's assassination eliminated a major secular political figure and deepened Pakistan's instability.

title: Benazir Bhutto assassinated in Pakistan
description: Former Prime Minister Benazir Bhutto was killed in a gun and bomb attack after an election rally in Rawalpindi. She was the first woman to lead a Muslim-majority nation and had returned from exile to contest elections.
sources: United Nations Commission of Inquiry Report 2010
outcome: Bhutto's assassination shocked the Muslim world and destabilized Pakistan's democratic transition.

title: Deaths in 2008 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 2008 CE: Mahmoud Darwish — poet (Foremost Palestinian poet and voice of Palestinian identity) in Haifa.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: Mumbai attacks by Lashkar-e-Taiba
description: Pakistani militant group Lashkar-e-Taiba carried out coordinated shooting and bombing attacks across Mumbai, India, killing 166 people. The attacks strained India-Pakistan relations and highlighted cross-border terrorism issues.
outcome: The attacks nearly derailed India-Pakistan peace efforts and intensified security cooperation demands.

title: Opening of Museum of Islamic Art in Doha completed
description: The Museum of Islamic Art designed by I.M. Pei officially opened in Doha, Qatar, housing one of the world's most comprehensive collections of Islamic art spanning 1,400 years. The museum's geometric limestone facade draws on Islamic architectural traditions while employing modernist design.
outcome: The museum established Qatar as a major cultural hub and model for preserving Islamic artistic heritage.

title: Turkey lifts headscarf ban in universities
description: Turkish parliament voted to lift the decades-old ban on headscarves in universities, a major victory for religious freedoms. The reform was championed by the AKP government under Prime Minister Erdoğan.
outcome: The reform represented a significant shift away from Kemalist secularism toward greater religious expression.

title: Turkey lifts headscarf ban in universities
description: Turkey's parliament voted to lift the decades-old ban on headscarves in universities, a key reform by the AKP government. The move reversed Kemalist restrictions and sparked debate about secularism and religious freedom.
outcome: The reform marked a significant shift in Turkish secularism and expanded religious expression in public institutions.

title: Deaths in 2009 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 2009 CE: Abdurrahman Wahid — ruler (Gus Dur — 4th President of Indonesia / Champion of Islamic Pluralism) in Jombang / Jakarta, Indonesia.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: Election protests in Iran (Green Movement)
description: Massive protests erupted in Iran after disputed presidential election results favoring Mahmoud Ahmadinejad over reformist Mir-Hossein Mousavi. The Green Movement represented the largest domestic challenge to the Islamic Republic since 1979.
outcome: The movement was suppressed but revealed deep political divisions within Iranian society.

title: Naguib Mahfouz dies, Arab literature's Nobel laureate
description: Egyptian novelist Naguib Mahfouz, who won the Nobel Prize for Literature in 1988, died at age 94 in Cairo. His Cairo Trilogy and dozens of other works explored Egyptian society, Islamic tradition, and modernization through literary realism.
outcome: Mahfouz's death marked the end of an era in Arabic literature and his works remain canonical in world literature.

title: Death of Grand Ayatollah Montazeri
description: Grand Ayatollah Hossein-Ali Montazeri, once designated successor to Khomeini and later a dissident critic of the Iranian regime, died in Qom. His funeral drew massive crowds supporting reform.
outcome: Montazeri's death galvanized the Green Movement and highlighted internal clerical opposition to the regime.

title: Death of Grand Ayatollah Montazeri
description: Grand Ayatollah Hossein-Ali Montazeri, once designated successor to Khomeini and later dissident critic of Iran's government, died under house arrest. He had challenged the concept of velayat-e faqih and defended human rights.
outcome: Montazeri's death was mourned by reformists and highlighted tensions between clerical authority and democratic governance in Iran.

title: Deaths in 2010 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 2010 CE: Mohammed Arkoun — scholar (Pioneer of Critical Islamic Studies) in Taourirt-Mimoun (Algeria) / Paris; Nasr Abu Zayd — scholar (Pioneer of Hermeneutic Approach to the Quran) in Tanta (Egypt) / Leiden; Abbas Hosseini Kashani — scholar (Mystic of Karbala) in Karbala.
outcome: 3 notable figures died this year.

title: Arab Spring begins in Tunisia
description: The self-immolation of Mohamed Bouazizi in Tunisia sparks mass protests that topple President Ben Ali, triggering uprisings across the Arab world from Egypt to Syria, Libya, Yemen and Bahrain.
outcome: Triggered the most significant political upheaval in the Arab world since decolonisation

title: Construction begins on Doha's Museum of Islamic Art
description: The Museum of Islamic Art in Doha, Qatar, designed by I.M. Pei, opened to international acclaim with collections spanning 1,400 years. The museum represented Gulf states' investment in cultural preservation and Islamic heritage.
outcome: The museum became a landmark of contemporary Islamic cultural institutions.

title: Mavi Marmara flotilla incident
description: Israeli naval forces raided the MV Mavi Marmara and other ships attempting to break the Gaza blockade, killing ten Turkish activists. The incident, involving the Turkish humanitarian organization IHH, severely damaged Israeli-Turkish relations.
outcome: The raid prompted international condemnation, a UN investigation, and years of diplomatic crisis between Israel and Turkey.

title: Floods devastate Pakistan, killing thousands
description: Catastrophic monsoon floods affected one-fifth of Pakistan's territory, killing nearly 2,000 people and displacing 20 million. The disaster prompted massive international humanitarian response.
outcome: The floods represented one of the worst natural disasters in Pakistani history and exposed governmental inadequacies.

title: Floods devastate Pakistan, killing thousands
description: Catastrophic monsoon floods affected over 20 million Pakistanis, killing nearly 2,000 and destroying vast agricultural land. The disaster became one of the worst humanitarian crises in Pakistan's history.
outcome: The floods exposed governance failures and triggered massive international humanitarian response.

title: Egyptian Revolution topples Mubarak
description: Eighteen days of mass protests in Tahrir Square force President Hosni Mubarak to resign after 30 years in power, briefly raising hopes for democratic governance across the Muslim world.
outcome: Briefly raised hopes for Arab democracy before a military coup in 2013 restored authoritarian rule

title: Gaddafi killed in Libyan civil war
description: Libyan dictator Muammar Gaddafi was captured and killed by rebel forces in Sirte after NATO-backed uprising. His death ended 42 years of authoritarian rule but plunged Libya into prolonged instability and civil conflict.
outcome: Libya descended into factional warfare and became a failed state.

title: Deaths in 2012 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 2012 CE: Ahmad Ghabel — scholar (Mystic of Torbat-e Jam) in Torbat-e Jam.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: Rohingya persecution intensifies in Myanmar
description: Communal violence erupted in Rakhine State, Myanmar, targeting the Muslim Rohingya minority and displacing over 100,000 people. The violence marked the beginning of what UN would later call genocide.
outcome: The crisis led to massive Rohingya refugee flows to Bangladesh and international condemnation.

title: Timbuktu manuscripts destroyed by Ansar Dine
description: Islamic extremist group Ansar Dine destroyed centuries-old Sufi shrines and manuscripts in Timbuktu, Mali, during their occupation. The destruction of this UNESCO World Heritage site shocked scholars of Islamic civilization.
outcome: The destruction highlighted threats to African Islamic heritage and led to ICC war crimes prosecutions.

title: Malian jihadists destroy Timbuktu shrines
description: Ansar Dine militants led by Iyad Ag Ghaly destroyed Sufi shrines and mausoleums in Timbuktu, including the tomb of Sidi Mahmoud Ben Amar. The destruction targeted centuries-old sites considered un-Islamic by the Salafi-jihadist group that briefly controlled northern Mali.
outcome: Ahmad al-Faqi al-Mahdi was later convicted by the International Criminal Court for war crimes related to the destruction.

title: Mohammed Morsi elected President of Egypt
description: Mohammed Morsi of the Muslim Brotherhood became Egypt's first democratically elected president following the Arab Spring. His presidency lasted only one year before military intervention.
outcome: Morsi's brief presidency represented the only democratic transition in Egypt before military restoration.

title: Mohammed Morsi elected President of Egypt
description: Muslim Brotherhood candidate Mohammed Morsi won Egypt's first free presidential election, defeating Ahmed Shafiq. His victory represented the Arab Spring's democratic promise but ended with military coup in 2013.
outcome: Morsi's brief presidency highlighted tensions between Islamist governance and military power in Egypt.

title: Deaths in 2013 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 2013 CE: Yousef Madani Tabrizi — scholar (Mystic of Tabriz) in Tabriz; Mohamed Said Ramadan Al-Bouti — scholar (Islamic Theology Scholar of Jilka) in Jilka; Muhammad Ali Siddiqui — scholar (Islamic Theology Scholar of Karachi) in Karachi.
outcome: 3 notable figures died this year.

title: Rabaa massacre in Cairo
description: Egyptian security forces violently dispersed Muslim Brotherhood sit-ins at Rabaa al-Adawiya Square, killing at least 817 protesters. The massacre followed the military coup that removed President Mohamed Morsi from power.
outcome: The massacre crushed the Muslim Brotherhood and consolidated military rule in Egypt.

title: Election of Rouhani as Iranian president
description: Hassan Rouhani won Iran's presidential election on a moderate platform, promising to negotiate nuclear deal and reduce international isolation. His victory signaled Iranian public desire for economic relief and engagement with the West.
outcome: Rouhani's presidency led to the 2015 Iran nuclear deal negotiations.

title: Rana Plaza factory collapse in Bangladesh
description: The eight-story Rana Plaza garment factory collapsed in Dhaka, killing 1,134 workers, mostly Muslim women, in Bangladesh's deadliest industrial disaster. The tragedy exposed labor exploitation in the global garment industry and sparked international reform efforts.
outcome: The disaster led to the Accord on Fire and Building Safety and global scrutiny of labor conditions in Muslim-majority manufacturing countries.

title: Gezi Park protests in Turkey
description: Protests against urban development in Istanbul's Gezi Park expanded into nationwide demonstrations against Erdoğan's government. The movement drew diverse opposition including secularists, Kurds, and liberal Muslims.
outcome: The protests marked a turning point toward more authoritarian governance in Turkey.

title: Gezi Park protests in Turkey
description: Mass protests erupted in Istanbul against government plans to demolish Gezi Park, evolving into nationwide demonstrations against Erdoğan's AKP government. Millions participated in what became Turkey's largest protests in decades.
outcome: The protests marked a turning point in Turkish politics, revealing deep divisions over secularism and authoritarianism.

title: Deaths in 2014 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 2014 CE: Abdelwahab Meddeb — scholar (Tunisian-French writer and critic of Islamic fundamentalism) in Paris.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: ISIS declares a 'caliphate' in Iraq and Syria
description: The Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIS) captures Mosul and declares a 'worldwide caliphate' under Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi. Muslim scholars worldwide condemn the declaration as illegitimate.
sources: Patrick Cockburn, The Rise of Islamic State
outcome: Provoked a global military response and a unified scholarly rejection of extremist theology

title: Muslim scholars issue Open Letter to ISIS
description: Over 120 leading Muslim scholars from around the world issue a detailed Open Letter to al-Baghdadi refuting ISIS ideology point by point using Islamic jurisprudence, the most comprehensive scholarly refutation of jihadist extremism.
outcome: Produced the most authoritative scholarly refutation of extremist ideology in Islamic history

title: Malala Yousafzai wins Nobel Peace Prize
description: Pakistani education activist Malala Yousafzai, who survived a Taliban assassination attempt in 2012, became the youngest Nobel laureate at age 17. She shared the prize with Indian child rights activist Kailash Satyarthi for their work on children's education rights.
outcome: Malala became a global symbol for girls' education and Muslim women's activism against extremism.

title: Abduction of Chibok schoolgirls by Boko Haram
description: Boko Haram militants abducted 276 female students from a school in Chibok, Nigeria, sparking global outrage and the #BringBackOurGirls campaign. Many remained in captivity for years.
outcome: The abduction highlighted Boko Haram's campaign of terror and the vulnerability of education in conflict zones.

title: Abduction of Chibok schoolgirls by Boko Haram
description: Boko Haram militants abducted 276 female students from a government secondary school in Chibok, Nigeria, sparking global #BringBackOurGirls campaign. Many remain missing or were forced into marriages.
outcome: The mass kidnapping exposed Boko Haram's brutality and Nigeria's security failures, galvanizing international attention.

title: Iran nuclear deal (JCPOA) signed
description: Iran and world powers (P5+1) signed the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action, limiting Iran's nuclear program in exchange for sanctions relief. Foreign Minister Javad Zarif led negotiations for Iran in this landmark diplomatic achievement.
outcome: The agreement temporarily eased tensions but collapsed after US withdrawal in 2018.

title: Saudi-led intervention in Yemen begins
description: Saudi Arabia launched military intervention in Yemen against Houthi rebels, leading a coalition of Arab states. The intervention created one of the world's worst humanitarian crises with millions facing famine.
outcome: The war created massive humanitarian crisis and regional proxy conflict.

title: Mass drownings of Muslim refugees in Mediterranean
description: Over 3,770 migrants and refugees, predominantly from Syria, Afghanistan, and North Africa, drowned attempting Mediterranean crossings to Europe in 2015. Single incidents included an April shipwreck killing an estimated 800 people off Libya's coast.
outcome: The crisis prompted European debates on refugee policy and highlighted the desperation of Muslims fleeing war and persecution.

title: Sisi opens New Suez Canal expansion
description: Egyptian President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi inaugurated a major expansion of the Suez Canal, allowing two-way traffic and reducing transit time. The project was completed in one year.
outcome: The expansion enhanced Egypt's strategic economic importance and maritime trade capacity.

title: Sisi opens New Suez Canal expansion
description: Egyptian President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi inaugurated the New Suez Canal expansion, adding a 35-kilometer parallel waterway to increase traffic capacity. The project was completed in one year as a nationalist achievement.
outcome: The expansion bolstered Egypt's economy and reinforced the canal's strategic importance to global maritime trade.

title: Deaths in 2016 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 2016 CE: Abbas Vaez-Tabasi — scholar (Mystic of Tabas) in Tabas; Taqi Tabatabaei Qomi — scholar (Mystic of Mashhad) in Mashhad; Geydar Dzhemal — philosopher (Philosopher of Moscow) in Moscow; Malek Chebel — philosopher (Philosopher of Skikda) in Skikda; Halil Inalcik — scholar (Foremost historian of the Ottoman Empire) in Ankara.
outcome: 5 notable figures died this year.

title: Failed coup attempt in Turkey
description: A faction within Turkish military attempted to overthrow President Recep Tayyip Erdogan, but was defeated by popular resistance and loyal forces. Erdogan blamed cleric Fethullah Gülen, leading to massive purges and state of emergency.
outcome: The failed coup consolidated Erdogan's power and transformed Turkey's political system.

title: Muhammad Ali dies as global Muslim icon
description: Boxing legend Muhammad Ali, born Cassius Clay, died at age 74 in Arizona after a decades-long battle with Parkinson's disease. His conversion to Islam in 1964 and refusal to serve in Vietnam made him a symbol of Black Muslim identity and conscientious objection.
outcome: Ali's funeral in Louisville drew world leaders and highlighted Islam's role in African American liberation movements.

title: Death of Muhammad Ali, global Muslim icon
description: Boxing legend Muhammad Ali died in Arizona, remembered globally as both a sports icon and advocate for Black Muslim identity and civil rights. His funeral in Louisville drew world leaders.
outcome: Ali's death prompted global reflection on Islam in America and Black Muslim heritage.

title: Deaths in 2017 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 2017 CE: Ali Asghar Mosleh — philosopher (Philosopher of Iran) in Iran.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: Mosul liberated from ISIS
description: Iraqi forces, backed by US-led coalition, recaptured Mosul from ISIS after nine-month battle, ending the group's territorial control in Iraq. The battle devastated the historic city including the destruction of the Great Mosque of al-Nuri.
outcome: The liberation marked the effective end of ISIS's territorial caliphate in Iraq.

title: Qatar diplomatic crisis
description: Saudi Arabia, UAE, Bahrain, and Egypt severed diplomatic ties with Qatar, imposing blockade and accusing it of supporting terrorism and ties with Iran. Emir Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani refused to comply with their demands.
outcome: The crisis reshaped Gulf politics until reconciliation in 2021.

title: Marawi siege in the Philippines
description: Maute group militants affiliated with ISIS seized the city of Marawi in Mindanao, leading to a five-month siege and battle with Philippine forces. The conflict killed over 1,100 people and displaced 350,000 residents of the predominantly Muslim city.
outcome: The siege demonstrated ISIS's reach into Southeast Asian Muslim communities and left Marawi in ruins requiring years of reconstruction.

title: Battle of Marawi in the Philippines
description: ISIS-affiliated militants seized Marawi City in the southern Philippines, leading to a five-month siege and urban warfare. Philippine forces eventually retook the city with heavy destruction.
outcome: The siege demonstrated ISIS's reach into Southeast Asian Muslim communities and left Marawi devastated.

title: Deaths in 2018 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 2018 CE: Fuat Sezgin — scholar (Turkish historian of Islamic science and author of Geschichte des arabischen Schrifttums) in Frankfurt.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: Assassination of Jamal Khashoggi
description: Saudi journalist Jamal Khashoggi was murdered inside the Saudi consulate in Istanbul by agents allegedly linked to Crown Prince Muhammad bin Salman. The killing provoked international outrage and scrutiny of Saudi leadership.
outcome: The assassination damaged Saudi Arabia's international reputation and strained US-Saudi relations.

title: Mahathir Mohamad returns as Malaysian Prime Minister
description: Mahathir Mohamad, 92, became the world's oldest elected leader by winning Malaysia's general election, ending the Barisan Nasional coalition's six-decade rule. His victory came amid corruption scandals involving Prime Minister Najib Razak and the 1MDB sovereign wealth fund.
outcome: The election marked a historic democratic transition in Muslim-majority Malaysia and led to Najib's corruption prosecution.

title: Mahathir Mohamad returns as Malaysian PM at 92
description: Mahathir Mohamad became the world's oldest head of government, returning as Malaysia's Prime Minister after leading opposition to defeat his former party in historic elections. He had previously served from 1981-2003.
outcome: Mahathir's return marked Malaysia's first democratic transfer of power since independence.

title: Deaths in 2019 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 2019 CE: Muhammad Talha Kandhlawi — scholar (Hadith Sciences Scholar of Kandhla) in Kandhla.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: Document on Human Fraternity signed in Abu Dhabi
description: The Grand Imam of Al-Azhar, Ahmad al-Tayyeb, and Pope Francis sign the Document on Human Fraternity for World Peace in Abu Dhabi, the most significant interfaith declaration between Christianity and Islam.
outcome: Established a new framework for Muslim-Christian dialogue endorsed at the highest levels of both faiths

title: Christchurch mosque shootings
description: White supremacist terrorist attacked two mosques in Christchurch, New Zealand, killing 51 Muslim worshippers during Friday prayers. The attack, livestreamed online, shocked the world and led to New Zealand gun law reforms.
outcome: The massacre highlighted rising Islamophobia and far-right extremism in Western countries.

title: Sudan's Omar al-Bashir ousted
description: Sudanese President Omar al-Bashir was overthrown by military after months of popular protests demanding democratic transition. The revolution ended 30 years of authoritarian Islamist rule in Sudan.
outcome: The revolution initiated Sudan's fragile transition toward civilian democratic rule.

title: Algeria's Bouteflika resigns after mass protests
description: Algerian President Abdelaziz Bouteflika resigned after two decades in power following massive Hirak protest movement demonstrations. The 82-year-old leader, largely absent from public view due to stroke, capitulated to weekly protests demanding democratic reform.
outcome: Bouteflika's fall continued the pattern of popular uprisings against long-serving Arab leaders but Algeria's military retained significant power.

title: India revokes Kashmir's special status
description: The Indian government under Narendra Modi revoked Article 370, ending Jammu and Kashmir's special autonomous status and imposing a military lockdown. The move sparked international concern over the Muslim-majority region.
outcome: The revocation fundamentally altered Kashmir's political status and intensified tensions with Pakistan.

title: India revokes Kashmir's special status
description: India's BJP government revoked Article 370 of the Constitution, ending Jammu and Kashmir's autonomy and statehood. The Muslim-majority region was placed under lockdown, sparking international concern and Pakistani protests.
outcome: The revocation fundamentally altered Kashmir's status and intensified tensions between India and Pakistan.

title: Abraham Accords normalize Israel-UAE-Bahrain relations
description: UAE and Bahrain normalized diplomatic relations with Israel through US-brokered Abraham Accords, breaking Arab consensus on Palestinian issue. The agreements reshaped Middle East diplomatic alignments.
outcome: The accords created new Gulf-Israeli alliance against Iran but divided Arab and Muslim opinion.

title: Hagia Sophia reconverted to mosque
description: Turkish President Erdogan ordered Hagia Sophia's conversion from museum back to mosque, 86 years after Atatürk secularized it. The decision symbolized Turkey's shift from secularism toward Islamic identity politics.
outcome: The conversion signaled Turkey's embrace of neo-Ottoman identity and troubled secularists.

title: Grand Imam and Pope sign Abrahamic Family House agreement
description: Grand Imam Ahmed el-Tayeb of al-Azhar and Pope Francis announced plans for the Abrahamic Family House complex in Abu Dhabi, featuring a mosque, church, and synagogue. The interfaith project built on their 2019 Document on Human Fraternity.
outcome: The project symbolized UAE's interfaith tolerance initiatives while critics noted restrictions on religious freedom in the Emirates.

title: Grand Mosque scaled to minimal Hajj pilgrims
description: Saudi Arabia limited Hajj to only 10,000 domestic pilgrims due to COVID-19 pandemic, down from over 2 million annually. This marked the most restricted Hajj in modern history.
outcome: The restrictions demonstrated unprecedented pandemic adaptation in Islamic pilgrimage practices.

title: Deaths in 2021 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 2021 CE: Abdalqadir as-Sufi — Sufi mystic (Founder of the Murabitun World Movement and author of numero) in Ayr; Wahiduddin Khan — scholar (Indian Islamic scholar and peace activist) in New Delhi; Hichem Djait — scholar (Tunisian historian of early Islam and Arab-Islamic civilisation) in Tunis.
outcome: 3 notable figures died this year.

title: Taliban retake Afghanistan as US withdraws
description: The Taliban seize control of Afghanistan in August as the United States completes its military withdrawal after 20 years of war, capturing Kabul in days as the Afghan government collapses.
sources: Carter Malkasian, The American War in Afghanistan
outcome: Ended the longest war in American history and raised urgent questions about Islamic governance and women's rights

title: Navalny protests draw Chechen and Tatar participation
description: Following the imprisonment of Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny, protests across Russia included significant participation from Muslim-majority regions including Tatarstan and Dagestan. Young Muslims in Kazan and Makhachkala joined calls for democratic reform and against corruption.
outcome: The protests demonstrated growing political activism among Russian Muslims, particularly youth, despite government repression.

title: Pakistan opens Kartarpur Corridor for Sikh pilgrims
description: Pakistan completed the Kartarpur Corridor allowing visa-free access for Indian Sikh pilgrims to Gurdwara Darbar Sahib, where Guru Nanak died in 1539. Prime Minister Imran Khan inaugurated the corridor as a gesture of religious tolerance and peace diplomacy.
outcome: The corridor eased Pakistan-India tensions temporarily and showcased Muslim-majority Pakistan's protection of non-Muslim heritage sites.

title: Ebrahim Raisi elected President of Iran
description: Hardliner Ebrahim Raisi won Iran's presidential election amid low turnout and disqualification of reform candidates. His election consolidated conservative control across Iranian institutions.
outcome: Raisi's presidency ended any pretense of reform and aligned executive with supreme leader's hardline policies.

title: Deaths in 2022 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 2022 CE: Yusuf al-Qaradawi — scholar (Most Influential Sunni Jurist of the 20th–21st Century) in Saft Turab (Egypt) / Doha, Qatar; Jawdat Said — philosopher (Philosopher of Beer Ajam) in Beer Ajam; Azyumardi Azra — scholar (Leading Indonesian Islamic scholar and historian of Southeast Asian Islam) in Jakarta.
outcome: 3 notable figures died this year.

title: Iran protests following Mahsa Amini's death
description: Mahsa Amini, a 22-year-old Kurdish woman, died in custody of Iran's morality police after arrest for allegedly violating hijab rules. Her death sparked nationwide protests with the slogan 'Woman, Life, Freedom' challenging the Islamic Republic's mandatory veiling laws.
outcome: The protests represented the most serious challenge to Iran's theocratic system in years, with particular participation from women and youth.

title: FIFA World Cup held in Muslim country for first time
description: Qatar hosted the FIFA World Cup, the first Muslim-majority country and first Middle Eastern nation to do so. The tournament sparked debates about migrant worker conditions, alcohol restrictions, and LGBTQ rights in Islamic contexts.
outcome: The World Cup showcased Qatari infrastructure investment while intensifying global scrutiny of Gulf labor systems and Islamic law in international events.

title: Louvre Abu Dhabi acquires major Islamic art collection
description: The Louvre Abu Dhabi acquired a significant collection of Islamic manuscripts and artifacts, enhancing its position as a major center for Islamic art preservation. The museum continued its mission of cultural bridge-building.
outcome: The acquisition strengthened the UAE's cultural diplomacy and Islamic heritage preservation efforts.

title: Deaths in 2023 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 2023 CE: Abbas-Ali Soleimani — scholar (Mystic of Esbu Kola) in Esbu Kola.
outcome: 1 notable figure died this year.

title: October 7 attack and Gaza war
description: Hamas launches a major attack on southern Israel on October 7, killing approximately 1,200 people. Israel responds with a devastating military campaign in Gaza that kills tens of thousands of Palestinians and triggers a global humanitarian crisis.
outcome: Reignited the Palestinian question as the central issue of Muslim geopolitics and provoked a worldwide debate about international law

title: Morocco earthquake devastates Atlas Mountains
description: A magnitude 6.8 earthquake struck Morocco's High Atlas Mountains, killing nearly 3,000 people and destroying historic villages and mosques. The disaster highlighted challenges facing rural Muslim communities and heritage preservation.
outcome: The earthquake prompted international aid and debate about preserving traditional Berber architecture.

title: Turkey and Syria earthquakes devastate Muslim communities
description: Massive earthquakes measuring 7.8 and 7.5 magnitude struck southern Turkey and northern Syria in February, killing over 50,000 people. The disaster particularly affected Syrian refugees and Turkish cities like Gaziantep and Kahramanmaraş.
outcome: The earthquakes caused one of the century's deadliest natural disasters, exposing building code violations and complicating Syrian conflict recovery.

title: Death of Ebrahim Raisi in helicopter crash
description: Iranian President Ebrahim Raisi died in a helicopter crash in mountainous terrain near the Azerbaijan border, along with Foreign Minister Hossein Amir-Abdollahian. The incident sparked succession questions in Iran.
outcome: Raisi's death created a leadership transition during a period of regional tension and domestic unrest.

title: Deaths in 2024 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 2024 CE: Fethullah Gulen — scholar (Hizmet Movement Leader — Most Influential Turkish Islamic Thinker) in Korucuk (Turkey) / Saylorsburg PA; Abdolnabi Namazi — scholar (Mystic of Bushehr) in Bushehr.
outcome: 2 notable figures died this year.

title: Fall of the Assad regime in Syria
description: In December 2024, opposition forces launch a major offensive that topples the Assad regime after 13 years of civil war, ending over 50 years of Assad family rule in Syria.
outcome: Ended decades of authoritarian rule and opened an uncertain new chapter for Syria and the wider region

title: Indonesia completes presidential election with peaceful transfer
description: Prabowo Subianto won Indonesia's presidential election, succeeding Joko Widodo in the world's largest Muslim-majority democracy. The peaceful democratic transition continued Indonesia's two-decade pattern of stable electoral politics following the end of authoritarian rule.
outcome: The election demonstrated the stability of Indonesian democracy and moderate Islam's compatibility with democratic governance.

title: Bangladesh's Sheikh Hasina flees amid mass protests
description: Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina fled to India after weeks of violent protests over job quotas evolved into demands for her resignation. Student-led movements toppled her 15-year rule.
outcome: Hasina's fall marked a dramatic shift in Bangladesh's political landscape after decades of Awami League dominance.

title: Indonesia inaugurates new capital Nusantara
description: Indonesia officially relocated its capital from Jakarta to the new purpose-built city of Nusantara in East Kalimantan. President Joko Widodo led the historic move affecting the world's largest Muslim-majority nation.
outcome: The new capital represented Indonesia's ambitious vision for decentralization and sustainable urban development.

title: Opening of Grand Egyptian Museum
description: Egypt fully opened the Grand Egyptian Museum near the Giza Pyramids, the world's largest archaeological museum. The facility showcases pharaonic heritage alongside Islamic Egyptian civilization exhibits.
outcome: The museum's opening marked a major milestone in Egypt's cultural tourism and heritage preservation strategy.

title: Senegal elects Bassirou Diomaye Faye as youngest African leader
description: Bassirou Diomaye Faye, 44, won Senegal's presidential election as an anti-establishment candidate, becoming Africa's youngest elected head of state. His victory represented a generational shift in West African Muslim politics.
outcome: Faye's election demonstrated youth political mobilization and democratic resilience in Muslim West Africa.

title: Deaths in 2025 CE
description: Notable deaths recorded in 2025 CE: Abdolkarim Soroush — scholar (Muslim Luther — Leading Islamic Reform Philosopher) in Tehran / Cambridge / Washington DC; Hisham Djait — scholar (Leading Tunisian Historian of Early Islam) in Tunis, Tunisia; Mohammad Khatami — ruler (Reform President of Iran — Dialogue of Civilizations) in Ardakan / Tehran, Iran; Seyyed Hossein Nasr — philosopher (Perennial Philosopher of Islam) in Tehran / Washington DC; Ziauddin Sardar — scholar (Leading British Muslim Thinker — Critical Muslim Editor) in Dipalpur / London; Khurshid Ahmad — philosopher (Philosopher of Delhi) in Delhi.
outcome: 6 notable figures died this year.
